items.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google has been actively releasing Gemini AI updates throughout early 2026. In March 2026, Google rolled out new Gemini app features focused on personalized context — including understanding travel plans, work projects, and shopping preferences — and expanded Search Live globally to over 200 countries. Gemini also received significant Workspace integrations for Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Drive, enabling AI-assisted document creation, spreadsheet generation, and cross-file search for Google AI Ult

items.title: Google DeepMind to open UK research lab for discovery of new materials — Silicon Republic
items.author: www.siliconrepublic.com
items.snippet: Google DeepMind has been prolific in early 2026, publishing across multiple research domains. A standout recent paper is on AlphaEvolve, an LLM-powered evolutionary coding agent that rewrites its own game theory algorithms — outperforming manually designed expert baselines in Counterfactual Regret Minimization (CFR) and Policy Space Response Oracles (PSRO). This represents a significant step in automated algorithm discovery where AI iteratively evolves and evaluates its own code. Another major t

items.title: Gemma 4: DeepMind's Most Capable Open Models for Reasoning | AIToolly
items.author: aitoolly.com
items.snippet: Google DeepMind's most prominent announcement in early April 2026 was the release of Gemma 4, unveiled on April 3, 2026. Gemma 4 represents Google's most capable open model family to date, optimized for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows. The models span a range from edge-deployable sizes up to 31B parameters and are released under the Apache 2.0 license, inviting researchers, developers, and institutions to build on top of them. The release emphasizes byte-for-byte efficiency and native s

items.title: Agent Factory Recap: Build an AI Workforce with Gemini 3 | Google Cloud Blog
items.author: cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Google Cloud has made several significant AI and Gemini announcements in early 2026. Most notably, Gemma 4 became available on Google Cloud on April 2, 2026 — built on the same research as Gemini 3, it offers context windows up to 256K tokens, native vision and audio processing, fluency in over 140 languages, and is released under a commercially permissive Apache 2.0 license. Also launching recently is Gemini 3.1 Pro on Vertex AI and Gemini CLI, representing a step forward in core reasoning capa

items.title: Image generation API | Generative AI on Vertex AI
items.author: cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Imagen 4 is Google's latest image generation model, available through both the Gemini API (via Google AI Studio / ai.google.dev) and Vertex AI on Google Cloud. The Gemini API path uses the `imagen-4.0-generate-preview-06-06` model ID and can be accessed via the `generateImages` endpoint, with Python and REST examples available in the official guide at ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/imagen. Authentication uses an API key for Gemini API or Application Default Credentials for Vertex AI. The model su

items.title: Leveling up your developer experience in Google AI Studio
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google AI Studio has introduced a major evolution with 'Google Antigravity,' a new agentic development platform launched in early 2026. Antigravity enables full-stack vibe coding — turning natural language prompts into production-ready apps with built-in Firebase integration (Cloud Firestore for databases, Firebase Authentication for secure sign-in), automatic package management (e.g., Framer Motion, Shadcn), and multiplayer/real-world service connectivity. The platform also added AGENTS.md rule

items.title: Google Summer of Code 2026 · google-gemini/gemini-cli · Discussion #23391
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The google-gemini GitHub organization has been actively releasing updates across its repositories in early 2026. The most notable project is gemini-cli, an open-source AI agent that brings Gemini capabilities directly into the terminal. Recent releases include v0.36.0-preview versions (around late March 2026) with support for Gemini Flash 3.1 Lite and improvements to core and UI features, as well as v0.35.0-preview versions with various enhancements. The project follows a structured release cade

items.title: Gemini API changelog April 1 2026 - Google AI Developers Forum
items.author: discuss.ai.google.dev
items.snippet: As of April 2026, the Gemini API has seen significant updates across models, features, and infrastructure. New preview models include gemini-2.5-flash-preview-04-17 (optimized for price-performance and adaptive thinking) and Gemini 3.1 Flash Image (gemini-3.1-flash-image) for high-quality image generation with improved pricing and latency. The Computer Use tool is now supported in gemini-3-pro-preview and gemini-3-flash-preview, enabling agentic use cases. Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS and 2.5 Pro TTS pr

items.title: GitHub - ssdeanx/a2a-agentkit: A2A AgentKit with Genkit and Gemini
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: Genkit is an open-source AI framework built and maintained by Google/Firebase, available for JavaScript/TypeScript (stable), Go (beta), and Python (alpha). It provides a code-centric approach to building AI-powered applications with support for text generation, structured output, streaming, tool calling, and agentic workflows. The framework includes a CLI and local developer UI for running, evaluating, and debugging AI flows, along with built-in observability and telemetry. Genkit supports multi
